skill.name: brainstorming
skill.description: Collaborative design exploration that refines ideas into validated specs through iterative questioning. Use before any creative work including creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior.
skill.summary: Turn rough product and engineering ideas into validated design specs through guided questions.
skill.author: CodingCossack

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography findings are false positives from ordinary planning language in SKILL.md. Markdown backticks were also misread as shell execution, but the skill does direct repository inspection, design-file creation, and a git commit, so publication should include a medium warning.

medium_findings.title: Repository File and Git Workflow Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to inspect project files, write a design document, and commit that document to git. This appears legitimate for planning, but it can change repository contents and history if followed without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly instruct repository review, file creation, and a git commit. The intent is a normal planning workflow, so this is a controlled operational risk rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown References Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged inline Markdown code spans and skill names as Ruby or shell backtick execution. No executable Ruby code, shell command, or command substitution syntax is present in the Markdown file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and the marked text is inline documentation syntax. I found no evidence of Ruby code or shell execution at those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography across normal prose about design, validation, commits, and planning. No cryptographic function, hash algorithm, encryption API, or security-sensitive computation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited location is natural-language guidance in SKILL.md. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.

content.user_title: Plan Better Features Through Guided Brainstorming
content.value_statement: Unclear ideas often lead to weak specs and avoidable rework. This skill guides focused questions, compares approaches, and turns decisions into a validated design.
actual_capabilities: Reviews current project context before shaping an idea.
Asks one focused question at a time to refine goals and constraints.
Compares two or three approaches with trade-offs and a recommendation.
Drafts design sections covering architecture, components, data flow, errors, and tests.
Can write the validated design to a dated document under docs/plans.
limitations: It does not implement the final design by itself.
It depends on accurate answers from the user.
It may need repository access to understand project context.
Git commits should be reviewed before they are created.

use_cases.title: Shape a New Feature
use_cases.description: Convert a rough product request into a clear feature design with goals, constraints, and success criteria.

use_cases.title: Compare Technical Approaches
use_cases.description: Explore architecture options before implementation and choose a path with documented trade-offs.

use_cases.title: Prepare Implementation Planning
use_cases.description: Create a validated design document that another planning or coding workflow can turn into tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Start From a Rough Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to help me refine this idea: [describe the idea]. Ask one question at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to define purpose, constraints, success criteria, and out-of-scope items for [feature or change].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Design Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for selecting an approach.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to propose two or three approaches for [problem]. Include trade-offs and your recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Validated Design Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to produce a validated design for [feature]. Cover architecture, components, data flow, error handling, and tests.

output_examples.input: We need a dashboard for account health, but the scope is vague.
output: A first clarification question about the dashboard audience.
A short comparison of possible dashboard scopes.
A staged design outline after the user confirms direction.

output_examples.input: Help us decide how to add notifications to an existing app.
output: Questions about notification channels, timing, and user controls.
Two or three implementation approaches with trade-offs.
A validated design section covering data flow and failure handling.

best_practices: Provide the project goal, current constraints, and known non-goals at the start.
Answer each question with concrete details or choose from the offered options.
Review the final design before allowing file writes or git commits.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to skip product or technical decision making.
Do not ask it to implement before the design is validated.
Do not allow automatic commits without reviewing the generated document.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps turn an early idea into a structured design through questions, options, and validation.

faq.question: Does it write production code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on discovery, design, and preparation before implementation.

faq.question: Why does it inspect project context?
faq.answer: It uses files, documentation, and recent commits to make the design fit the existing project.

faq.question: Can it create files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can write a validated design document under docs/plans when the workflow reaches that step.

faq.question: Can it commit changes?
faq.answer: The skill instructs a git commit for the design document. Users should review the document before committing.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
